title: Beginning, baptism, calling disciples, and healings
summary_text: The gospel begins with Isaiah’s voice and John baptizing in the wilderness; Jesus is baptized, the Spirit descends, and the Father speaks. Satan tempts Jesus forty days; John is arrested; Jesus preaches the Kingdom and calls Simon, Andrew, James, and John. In Capernaum he teaches with authority, casts out an unclean spirit, heals Peter’s mother-in-law and many sick and demonized, prays alone, and cleanses a leper who must show the priest.
key_points: John preached repentance and baptism for forgiveness.
The Spirit drove Jesus into the wilderness after baptism.
Jesus called fishers to become fishers of men.
He cast out demons with authority, not as the scribes.
The leper was told to offer what Moses commanded for cleansing.
evidence_phrases: The beginning of the Good News of Jesus Christ
You are my beloved Son
The time is fulfilled, and God’s Kingdom is at hand
What is this? A new teaching with authority!
if you are willing, you can make me clean

title: Paralytic forgiven, Levi, fasting, Lord of the Sabbath
summary_text: A paralytic lowered through the roof is forgiven and healed to show the Son of Man’s authority on earth. Jesus calls Levi at the tax office and eats with sinners as the physician for the sick, teaching new wine needs new wineskins. Disciples pluck grain on the Sabbath; Jesus cites David and declares the Son of Man Lord of the Sabbath, then heals a withered hand on the Sabbath as lawful to do good.
key_points: Which is easier—to forgive sins or say arise and walk?
Jesus ate with tax collectors and sinners.
Fasting comes when the bridegroom is taken.
The Sabbath was made for man, not man for the Sabbath.
It is lawful to do good on the Sabbath.
evidence_phrases: the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins
I came not to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance
new wine must be put into new wineskins
The Sabbath was made for man
Is it lawful on the Sabbath day to do good?

title: Healing, the Twelve, unpardonable sin, true family
summary_text: Jesus heals on the Sabbath in the synagogue; Pharisees and Herodians plot to destroy him. Multitudes follow; he appoints twelve apostles to preach and cast out demons. Scribes from Jerusalem accuse him of Beelzebul; he warns about blasphemy against the Holy Spirit. His mother and brothers seek him; he defines his true family as those who do God’s will.
key_points: Jesus appointed twelve to be with him and send to preach.
A kingdom divided against itself cannot stand.
Blasphemy against the Holy Spirit has no forgiveness forever.
Jesus’s brother and sister and mother are those doing God’s will.
evidence_phrases: The Pharisees went out, and immediately conspired with the Herodians
he appointed twelve
How can Satan cast out Satan?
whoever blasphemes against the Holy Spirit never has forgiveness
whoever does the will of God, the same is my brother

title: Parables by the sea and calming the storm
summary_text: Jesus teaches from a boat: the sower, the purpose of parables, the lamp on a stand, the growing seed, and the mustard seed. With the disciples he interprets the sower as varied soils receiving the word. That evening they cross; a great windstorm threatens the boat; Jesus rebukes the wind and sea and asks why they have no faith, astonishing them at his authority.
key_points: The word is snatched, withers, choked, or bears fruit.
To outsiders are parables; to disciples the mystery is given.
Take heed what you hear—with what measure you measure.
The mustard seed becomes greatest of herbs.
Even wind and sea obey him.
evidence_phrases: A sower went out to sow
To you has been given the mystery of God’s Kingdom
Take heed what you hear
mustard seed
Peace! Be still!

title: Gerasene demoniac, Jairus’s daughter, bleeding woman
summary_text: Jesus casts Legion into swine that drown; the delivered man is sent to tell his friends. Jairus begs for his daughter; a woman touches Jesus’s garment and is healed of twelve years’ bleeding. Messengers say the girl died; Jesus says fear not, believe, raises her, and tells them to give her something to eat.
key_points: The demoniac lived among tombs and broke chains.
Jesus permitted the demons to enter about two thousand swine.
The woman was made well by faith in touching his clothes.
Jesus took the child by the hand and told her to arise.
He commanded silence then told them to feed the girl.
evidence_phrases: My name is Legion, for we are many
Go to your house, to your friends
Who touched my clothes?
Daughter, your faith has made you well
Talitha cumi!
give her something to eat

title: Rejection, mission, John’s death, feeding and walking on water
summary_text: Nazareth rejects Jesus as the carpenter’s son; he sends the twelve two by two with authority over unclean spirits. Herod fears John is raised; the narrative tells John’s beheading for Herodias’s daughter’s request. Jesus feeds five thousand with five loaves and two fish, walks on the sea, and stills disciples’ fear; many are healed in Gennesaret.
key_points: Jesus marveled at Nazareth’s unbelief.
The twelve anointed the sick and cast out demons.
Herodias’s daughter asked for John’s head on a platter.
Twelve baskets remained after feeding the crowd.
Jesus told terrified disciples, I am; don’t be afraid.
evidence_phrases: A prophet is not without honor, except in his own country
they cast out many demons
Bring me John the Baptizer’s head
They all ate, and were filled
he came to them, walking on the sea
It is I. Don’t be afraid

title: Traditions, Syrophoenician faith, deaf and mute healed
summary_text: Pharisees and scribes challenge unwashed hands; Jesus quotes Isaiah on vain worship and teaches what defiles comes from within the heart. A Gentile woman’s faith overcomes his children’s-bread saying and heals her daughter. He heals a deaf mute in Decapolis, charging them to tell no one—yet they publish it widely.
key_points: Traditions can void God’s commandment.
Evil thoughts and covetousness defile a person.
Even the dogs under the table eat children’s crumbs.
Jesus said great is your faith; the daughter was healed.
He sighed and said Ephphatha to open ears and tongue.
evidence_phrases: This people honors me with their lips
There is nothing from outside of the man
even the dogs under the table eat the children’s crumbs
For this saying, go your way. The demon has gone out of your daughter
Ephphatha!
He has done all things well

title: Four thousand fed, yeast, blind man, Peter’s confession
summary_text: Jesus feeds four thousand with seven loaves; Pharisees seek a sign and are refused. Disciples misunderstand leaven warnings. At Bethsaida a blind man is healed gradually. At Caesarea Philippi Peter confesses Christ; Jesus foretells suffering, death, and resurrection, rebukes Peter as savoring human things, and teaches taking up the cross to save life by losing it.
themes: Who do you say I am?
key_points: Seven baskets of fragments remained after four thousand ate.
No sign shall be given to this generation.
Peter said you are the Christ.
Jesus called Peter Satan for rebuking the suffering message.
Whoever loses his life for Jesus’s sake will save it.
evidence_phrases: They ate and were filled. They took up seven baskets full
no sign will be given to this generation
You are the Christ
Get behind me, Satan!
whoever will lose his life for my sake

title: Transfiguration, boy with spirit, greatness and salt
summary_text: Jesus is transfigured with Moses and Elijah; the Father says hear him. Coming down, they find disciples unable to cast out a boy’s spirit; Jesus rebukes the unclean spirit after the father’s belief cry. They dispute greatness; Jesus sets a child in their midst. Strange exorcist is not forbidden. Severe warnings against causing little ones to stumble; salt and peace.
themes: This kind comes out with prayer
key_points: A cloud voice said this is my beloved Son; listen to him.
The spirit convulsed the boy until Jesus cast it out.
Whoever receives a child in Jesus’s name receives him.
Whoever is not against us is on our side.
Everyone will be salted with fire.
evidence_phrases: This is my beloved Son. Listen to him.
I believe. Help my unbelief!
Whoever receives one of such little children in my name
For whoever is not against us is on our side
Have salt in yourselves

title: Marriage, children, rich man, cup, and Bartimaeus
summary_text: Jesus teaches God’s marriage design and blesses little children, saying the Kingdom belongs to such. A rich man goes away grieved; Jesus says how hard for the rich to enter God’s Kingdom, yet with God all things are possible. He foretells his suffering and James and John’s request for glory; greatness is service. Blind Bartimaeus by the road receives sight for faith.
key_points: What God joined man must not separate.
The Kingdom belongs to children like these.
It is easier for a camel to go through a needle’s eye.
The Son of Man came to give his life a ransom for many.
Bartimaeus followed Jesus in the way after healing.
evidence_phrases: What therefore God has joined together
the Kingdom of God belongs to such as these
How hard it is for those who have riches to enter
the Son of Man also came … to give his life as a ransom
Go your way. Your faith has made you well

title: Entry, fig tree, temple, and authority
summary_text: Jesus enters Jerusalem on a colt with Hosannas; he curses a fig tree for leaves without fruit. He drives buyers and sellers from the temple as a house of prayer for all nations. The fig tree withers; Jesus teaches prayer with forgiveness. Chief priests, scribes, and elders question his authority; he answers with John’s baptism.
key_points: They spread cloaks and branches on the road.
Jesus quoted Isaiah on a house of prayer for all nations.
Have faith in God for removing mountains.
Forgive others so your Father forgives you.
They could not say where John’s baptism was from.
evidence_phrases: Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord!
My house will be called a house of prayer
Have faith in God
whenever you stand praying, forgive
We don’t know

title: Vineyard, Caesar, resurrection, and the great commandment
summary_text: The vineyard parable shows tenants killing the beloved son; the rejected stone becomes the head of the corner. Pharisees and Herodians test the coin; Jesus says render to Caesar and to God. Sadducees fail on the resurrection; God is God of the living. A scribe agrees love sums the law; Jesus says he is not far from the Kingdom. Jesus asks how David’s son is his Lord.
key_points: The heir was cast out of the vineyard and killed.
Render to Caesar the things that are Caesar’s.
God is not the God of the dead, but of the living.
Love God and neighbor is more than burnt offerings.
David calls him Lord in the Spirit.
evidence_phrases: They took him, killed him, and cast him out
Render to Caesar
He is not the God of the dead, but of the living
You are not far from God’s Kingdom
David himself said in the Holy Spirit

title: Olivet signs and watchfulness
summary_text: Jesus foretells the temple’s stones cast down; disciples ask when. He warns of false christs, wars, earthquakes, gospel to all nations, and abomination of desolation flight. After tribulation the Son of Man comes in clouds gathering the elect. The fig tree lesson and unknown day and hour call for watchfulness as servants and virgins await the master.
key_points: Not one stone will be left on another.
The gospel must first be preached to all nations.
Pray flight be not in winter.
The Son of Man will come in clouds with great glory.
No one knows the day or hour—only the Father.
evidence_phrases: not one stone here will be left on another
This Good News must first be preached
when you see the abomination of desolation
then they will see the Son of Man coming in clouds
no one knows, not even the angels in heaven
Watch therefore

title: Anointing, Passover, Gethsemane, and arrest
summary_text: The chief priests plot; a woman anoints Jesus for burial amid objection. Judas betrays. Jesus institutes the Passover bread and cup as his body and blood of the new covenant. Peter denies thrice foretold; Gethsemane prayer yields not my will but yours. Judas betrays with a kiss; disciples flee; a young man flees naked; trials begin before the high priest with false witnesses.
themes: She has done a good work
key_points: She has anointed his body beforehand for burying.
This is my blood of the new covenant shed for many.
The spirit is willing but the flesh is weak.
Judas called him Rabbi and kissed him.
Peter followed at a distance and denied at the fire.
evidence_phrases: she has done a good work for me
This is my blood of the new covenant
not what I desire, but what you desire
Rabbi!
before the rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times

title: Before Pilate, crucifixion, and burial
summary_text: Jesus is bound before Pilate; the crowd chooses Barabbas and shouts crucify him. Soldiers mock, scourge, and crucify him with the inscription King of the Jews; darkness covers the land; Jesus cries forsaken and breathes his last; the veil tears; the centurion says truly this was God’s Son. Women watch; Joseph of Arimathaea buries him in a tomb sealed with a stone.
key_points: Pilate released Barabbas and delivered Jesus.
They crucified him between two robbers.
From the sixth hour there was darkness until the ninth.
Jesus cried with a loud voice and yielded up his spirit.
Joseph asked for the body and laid him in a tomb.
evidence_phrases: Crucify him!
The King of the Jews
My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?
the veil of the temple was torn in two
Truly this man was the Son of God!
he gave up his spirit

title: Empty tomb, appearances, and commission
summary_text: Women find the tomb open; a young man in white announces Jesus has risen and sends word to Peter and the disciples to meet him in Galilee. Mary Magdalene sees him; two on the road report; eleven are rebuked for unbelief. Jesus commissions preaching the gospel to every creature with signs following, ascends to the Father’s right hand, and disciples go forth preaching while the Lord works with them.
key_points: The stone was rolled away for the women at sunrise.
Go tell his disciples and Peter he goes before you to Galilee.
Upbraided them for unbelief and hardness of heart.
Whoever believes and is baptized will be saved.
Signs include casting out demons, new tongues, and laying on hands.
The Lord worked with them confirming the word.
evidence_phrases: He has risen! He is not here
tell his disciples and Peter
he rebuked their unbelief and hardness of heart
He who believes and is baptized will be saved
they will speak with new languages
the Lord working with them
